fixes_applied: Replaced tests/test.sh: was the SWE-rebench-style auto-generated stub (FAIL_TO_PASS=[], PASS_TO_PASS=[]) which falls back to `go test -v ./...` overall pass rate. At base commit febf309b that command produces a high pass rate on a no-op agent (the test suite was healthy pre-patch and the canonical patch only refactors call sites and extracts a helper), so virtually every trial scored ~1.0 — confirmed-fatal degenerate-ceiling trap (overturns prior KEEP verdict). New test.sh runs 6 inline source-grep / brace-walking behavioral gates against the patched repo, builds /logs/verifier/gates.json, and emits a weighted-replace reward.

Rewrote tests/test_manifest.yaml: aligned 1:1 with the new test.sh gates, weight sum 1.00, no P2P_REGRESSION declared (the touched files have hook-registry tests in the buggy commit that the canonical patch's instruction explicitly notes 'may need updates' — running them as a regression floor would risk false-zeroing on alt-fix shapes that rewrite hook tests differently).

Anti-overfitting design: gates target behavioral landmarks (DiscoverAndRegister call-site depth, presence of Args:cobra.ArbitraryArgs + RunE on the hooks command, an executeAgentHook-named helper with substantive body, thin verb RunE, setup.go imports/calls external, limitedWriter error-wrap pattern) rather than exact identifiers from the canonical patch. The G3 helper-extracted gate matches any function name containing 'executeAgentHook' and uses an anti-stub marker count (>=3 of 5 marker symbols) so a placeholder doesn't pass.
f2p_added: g1_no_eager_discovery (weight 0.25) - DiscoverAndRegister no longer called at function-body top level inside newHooksCmd in hooks_cmd.go (Python brace-walker extracts the function body and verifies all DiscoverAndRegister calls are at depth >= 1, i.e. nested in RunE/anonymous func)

g2_hooks_runE (weight 0.20) - hooks_cmd.go's newHooksCmd cobra.Command literal declares both `Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs` and a `RunE: func(...)` handler (the Cobra fallback mechanism for unrecognized subcommands)

g3_executeAgentHook_extracted (weight 0.20) - hook_registry.go defines a top-level function whose name contains 'executeAgentHook' with a substantive body referencing >=3 of {WorktreeRoot, IsEnabled, ParseHookEvent, DispatchLifecycleEvent, currentHookAgentName} (anti-stub guard against 1-line placeholders)

g4_thin_verb_runE (weight 0.10) - hook_registry.go's newAgentHookVerbCmdWithLogging RunE body is now thin (<=8 substantive lines, excluding comments and braces), evidencing delegation to a helper rather than the ~60 lines of inline logic in the buggy state

g5_setup_imports_external (weight 0.15) - cmd/entire/cli/setup.go both imports the agent/external package AND calls external.DiscoverAndRegister (so externals are discoverable during `entire enable`); the buggy state does neither

g6_limitedwriter_err_check (weight 0.10) - external.go's limitedWriter.Write body contains all of: a buf.Write call, an `err != nil` check, and a fmt.Errorf wrap (canonical replaces the plain `return w.buf.Write(p)` with proper error propagation)
notes:
Smoking-gun validation performed. Cloned entireio/cli @ febf309b5a54770f084a3028c31df17f54205a0f to /tmp/cli-test, ran the new test.sh against (a) the buggy baseline, (b) the canonical-patch-applied state, (c) two partial-fix scenarios.

Buggy baseline (no agent work):
  g1_no_eager_discovery          -> fail (DiscoverAndRegister at hooks_cmd.go top level, depth 0)
  g2_hooks_runE                  -> fail (no RunE on hooks command)
  g3_executeAgentHook_extracted  -> fail (no such function)
  g4_thin_verb_runE              -> fail (~60 substantive lines in inline RunE)
  g5_setup_imports_external      -> fail (setup.go has no agent/external import)
  g6_limitedwriter_err_check     -> fail (plain `return w.buf.Write(p)`)
  Final reward: 0.0000

Canonical-patched (full canonical patch from data-pipeline/artifacts_cli/canonical_patches/dbd2bfe1-...json applied via `git apply`):
  All 6 gates -> pass
  Final reward: 1.0000

Partial-fix scenarios:
  - Core refactor only (G1+G2+G3+G4 - the actual primary deliverable per instruction.md, skipping the tangential setup.go discovery and external.go err-handling): 0.7500
  - Full canonical minus the limitedWriter cosmetic change (5 of 6 gates): 0.9000
  - Only the limitedWriter fix (1 gate, ignores the actual task): 0.1000

This 0.00 -> 1.00 spread is sharp and the partial-credit profile rewards the primary refactor over the tangential cosmetic fix. The previous SWE-rebench stub gave near-1.00 on a no-op since `go test -v ./...` passes on a clean checkout. The earlier KEEP verdict noted 'empty FAIL_TO_PASS is correct because the patch adds no new tests' - that's the trap: with no F2P, the SWE-rebench template falls back to overall pass rate, which on a healthy unit-test suite scores near 1.0 for any agent (including doing nothing).

WHY THIS IS A KEEPER: behavioral gates (call-site depth check, Cobra field declaration, helper extraction with anti-stub marker count, thin RunE delegation, import+call presence, error-wrap pattern) are implementation-agnostic in the way that matters: the gates accept any function name containing 'executeAgentHook' and don't require specific argument signatures, ordering of checks, or particular error-message wording. The G1 brace-walker is a Python AST-light implementation that handles strings/comments correctly, so it won't false-positive on a literal 'DiscoverAndRegister' inside a string. The instruction.md is rich (101 lines including a code template), the canonical patch is 113/-71 lines across 4 files, and the multi-turn signal comes from the user_simulation_prompt.md driving correction loops on the helper extraction and the test-update follow-through.

NOTE ON P2P_REGRESSION: deliberately omitted. The canonical-patch instruction notes hook_registry_test.go 'may need updates' since tests call newHooksCmd(); a regression floor that runs go test ./cmd/entire/cli/... would risk false-zeroing on alt-fix shapes that legitimately update those tests differently. Per CLAUDE.md/scoring_traps.md, P2P should only be declared when the gate represents a genuine regression check that cannot be tripped by valid alt-fixes.
